description: The crate-level doc (lines 39-41) and all three try_sign doc comments (Ed25519Signer line 142, EcdsaP256Signer line 200, RsaPssSigner line 266) state: 'Each call creates a scoped thread with a current-thread tokio runtime.' This is false for the non-runtime code path. In sign_sync (lib.rs:64-73), when Handle::try_current().is_err() the function returns immediately after block_on on the calling thread — no thread is spawned at all. Only the async-runtime path (lines 75-93) spawns a scoped thread. A caller relying on the documented thread-isolation guarantee (e.g. for stack depth, thread-local state, or cancellation isolation) will be wrong in the common sync caller case. Fix: change the doc to accurately describe both paths: 'If called within a tokio runtime, spawns a scoped thread; otherwise runs on the calling thread.'
title: sign_sync doc claims scoped thread always; non-runtime path runs inline
